repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 has only Manski bounded-outcome wrappers; no policy-score support-function bridge

published_prior_art.cite: CinelliHazlett2020 (https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/82/1/39/7056023), ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 (https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/81/4/735/7048357), AdjahoChristensen2025 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.05561): support-function ingredients, not the combined finite-policy calibration display

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: sharp joint-misspecification variational interval and non-rectangularity witness, not a CH/MSM/covariate-shift score-calibration frontier

published_prior_art.cite: JinXuZhengZhou2025 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.20550) and AdjahoChristensen2025 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.05561): closest robust policy criteria; neither gives the all-pairs tau_Pi* scalar-calibration threshold

HuangPimentel2025 (https://academic.oup.com/biomet/article/doi/10.1093/biomet/asae040/7731116) and DiegertMastenPoirier2025 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.21106): close scalar-sensitivity comparators, but no open-set two-policy score-shape witness

strengthening_paths.suggestion: In Stage 0, make the bridge from the local support functions to the exact Jin-Xu-Zheng-Zhou MSM welfare criterion explicit, so the result is not merely a rank-one norm comparison after an unstated normalization.
Add a finite-support worked example computing Q_Pi*, tau_Pi*, and the resulting divergent choice sets for two policies; this will make the non-equivalence frontier inspectable.

If aiming beyond the local result, state a nonlocal sharp-bound version where the CH, MSM, and covariate-shift ambiguity sets are the original published sets rather than their first-order support-function reductions.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0, with the local-to-published-criterion normalization treated as the main derivation burden.
